# Transcrição: Dare_to_be_DULL

- **Fonte:** Dare_to_be_DULL
- **Duração:** 02:50
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 02:50]

I want to be a part of it, New York, New York. I just recently did something really scary. I spoke in front of 400 professional speakers. They're my peers. It was the scariest thing I've ever done in my life. You can put me in front of 5,000 people and I'm not scared. But you put me in front of 400, 300 of my fellow professional speakers and thinking about it, I can't even talk properly. I was pacing around backstage like crazy. My mouth went completely dry. I was sweating bullets. And I thought about, well, why the hell did that happen to me? It's because I wanted to be perfect. I had this deep desire that when I went out on stage, everything was going to be perfect. I wouldn't make one mistake. And do you know what happened? I didn't give my best performance. I don't believe I gave it everything I got. I knew I had more in me, but that wanton desire to be perfect crippled me. And it reminded me of a lesson that my theater coach used to teach our class. He would always say, dare to be dull. And it always sounds really strange. I mean, why would you want to be dull? But he always explained to us that in order to be great, you must dare to be dull. And the example he gave us was he said, actors and actresses, because they wanted to be perfect, that actually sometimes weren't even auditioned for a position. They go, I'm not good enough yet to try. I'm not good enough yet to go there and audition for that role. And then as a result, they never even try. There are so many of us in life, including myself and maybe you too, who's watching this right now, that we want to be perfect so bad that we never even try to reach for the goals that we desire, the positions that are at work that we want to try for, the person that we love that we want to tell about, because we feel we're not good enough, we don't even ever tell them how we feel. Daring to be dull is not a bad thing. It's not you being boring. It's not you being a chicken. It's actually you being courageous. I meet so many people who have so many ideas that they want to try. They've said business ideas. Some of them have hobbies they want to pursue. They have new talents they want to explore and they become so scared to even try. You know what the craziest thing is? When I spoke to my fellow speakers and my fellow buddies, everyone had nothing but praise for me. Everyone had nothing but support and love for me. All the negative thoughts, all the negative comments were just in my own head. And I think a lot of the times we need to get out of our own way in order for us to succeed. It's not even about the people out there who are saying negative things to you. I think the first person that we have to push out of the way is ourselves. So my message is clear and simple. If it's worth doing, it's worth doing badly. Thank you.
